item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Protecting Pacific Taro
item.description: [Education News PNG] USP Researcher Tackles Climate Challenges As the world marks World Rural Development Day on 6 July, attention turns to the vital role rural communities play The post Protecting Pacific Taro first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok Warrior
item.description: [Inside PNG] Colonel Aruma witnessed history as PNGDF mortars roared on Australian soil, marking a major milestone for the bilateral defense partnership. The post PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok Warrior first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok

item.title: NSW Blues Complete State of Origin Series Sweep with Dominant Game 3 Victory
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The New South Wales Blues produced a commanding performance to defeat the Queensland Maroons 30-12 in Game 3 of the Ampol State of Origin series at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane on Wednesday. Played in fine weather on good ground conditions, the Blues showed their class from start to finish to close o

item.title: Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant gold smuggling issues, undermining its economy and reducing revenue for essential services. Efforts are being made to enhance monitoring and disrupt smuggling networks for better resource management. The post Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAW

item.title: PNG Air Niugini Restarts Direct Port Moresby–Tokyo Flights After Six-Year Break
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's national airline will once again connect PNG directly with Japan, with Air Niugini confirming that nonstop services between Port Moresby and Tokyo will resume from July 18, 2026. The return of the route restores a key international link that has been absent since 2020 and is expec

item.title: Prime Minister Marape says PNG–Australia Defence Treaty elevates relationship to a new level
item.description: [Post Courier] Prime Minister James Marape says the Papua New Guinea-Australia Defence Treaty marks a new chapter in the relationship between the two countries, elevating it from a traditional bilateral partnership to a broader strategic partnership grounded in shared history, democratic values, economic cooperati

item.title: Maladina assures broader representation at Papua LNG Development Forum
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By DALCY LULUA PETROLEUM Minister Jimmy Maladina has sought to reassure communities in Gulf and Central provinces that they will have a voice at the upcoming Papua LNG Development Forum, saying the government has expanded district representation to ensure broader participation in discussions on the

item.title: Australian Partnership helps PNG Cocoa farmers access premium markets
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea cocoa farmers are receiving Australian support to meet international sustainability standards and secure access to premium overseas markets through a partnership with leading cocoa exporter NGIP-Agmark. The initiative, launched to coincide with World Chocolate Day, is helping farmer

item.title: Ol ami rausim planti bom long East New Britain provins
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol bom ekspet na sapot opisa bilong dispela wok i kam long Australia, PNG, New Zealand, Canada, France, Japan, UK na USA i bin stap insait long wanpela operesen we ol i rausim inap 2,200 bom bilong Wol Woa 2 long East New Britain provins long Jun dispela yia. Australia i go pas long dispela wok anin

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World University Innovation category ranking
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has strengthened its standing among the world’s internationally recognized universities after climbing more than 400 places in the The post USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World Univ

item.title: Air Niugini Returns to Japan
item.description: [Inside PNG] Air Niugini resumes direct Port Moresby-Tokyo flights on July 18, halving travel times and boosting trade, tourism, and regional connectivity. The post Air Niugini Returns to Japan first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Air Niugini Returns to Japan appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Leader Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific "Ocean of Peace" After Chinese Missile Test
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has urged major military powers across the globe to honour the Pacific as a zone of peace, after China fired a long-range ballistic missile into open waters in the South Pacific region as part of a test launch, directly affecting waters close to Papua New Guinea and its n

item.title: TVET and Universities in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's education system offers two main pathways: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) focuses on practical skills for immediate employment, while universities emphasise theoretical knowledge and long-term career preparation. The post TVET and Universities in Papua New

item.title: PNG and Australia Enter Historic Pukpuk Alliance Treaty
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea and Australia have officially become formal treaty allies after the Pukpuk Treaty came into legal effect today, marking a new chapter in relations between the two neighbouring countries. The agreement became legally binding following the exchange of instruments of ratification, comp

item.title: ON THIS DAY IN OUR HISTORY: First election challenge filed
item.description: [Post Courier] The runner-up in the Madang Provincial seat, Mr Peter Yama, yesterday petitioned the Supreme Court to declare that a 1991 parliamentary amendment to the Organic Law on National Elections was unconstitutional. The post ON THIS DAY IN OUR HISTORY: First election challenge filed appeared first on Post

item.title: US, PNG Defence Forces to hold joint military exercise
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PAPUA New Guinea and the United States will strengthen defence cooperation next week when they begin Exercise Tamiok Strike 2026, a joint military training exercise involving about 175 personnel. The exercise will be held from July 13 to 24 in Port Moresby, Wewak, and Lae, with the participation of

item.title: PNG congratulates Solomon Islands on 48th Independence
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has congratulated the Government and people of the Solomon Islands on the country’s 48th Independence Anniversary, reaffirming Papua New Guinea’s commitment to strengthening its longstanding Melanesian partnership with its Pacific neighbor. In a statement marking the

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment
item.description: [Inside PNG] PPL will appeal a major court-ordered payment. Executives cite severe legal errors, vowing a fierce challenge to protect company assets. The post PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: Monetisation of Online Content in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] The monetisation of online content in Papua New Guinea is growing, with efforts to enhance infrastructure and access for creators on platforms like YouTube, Facebook, and Instagram amid policy challenges. The post Monetisation of Online Content in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: NSW Blues Clinch State of Origin Series with Convincing Game Three Win
item.description: [PNG Facts] New South Wales have been crowned the 2026 Ampol State of Origin champions after producing a commanding performance to defeat Queensland 30-12 in the deciding third match at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane on Wednesday night. The Blues outplayed the Maroons across both halves, scoring five tries to thre

item.title: BSP response to North Fly MP’s statement on Kiunga branch operation
item.description: [Post Courier] BSP acknowledges the importance of banking services to the Kiunga township and the North Fly District's important role in supporting the development of Western Province, including existing and planned resource projects. This is in respond to North Fly MP James Donald has called on BSP Bank to improv

item.title: Censorship Office first to graduate under Finance Department’s Blended PFMA Training
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] TEN officers from the Office of Censorship have graduated as the first recipients of the Department of Finance’s new blended online learning program on the Public Finance Management Act (PFMA). The ceremony marked a dual achievement for the Office of Censorship, as the first national agency to compl

item.title: Solomon Airlines resumes Port Moresby-Honiara flights after 15-year break
item.description: [The PNG Sun] As Solomon Islands is celebrating its 48th Independence anniversary, the Solomon Airlines has resumed direct flights between Port Moresby and Honiara after a 15-year hiatus. This direct flight has restored weekly air services aimed at boosting tourism, trade, business, people-to-people and regional

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: Fight at Paglum Adventist Secondary School Leaves 16 Students Injured, Classes Suspended
item.description: [Education News PNG] Western Highlands, Papua New Guinea – Classes at Paglum Adventist Secondary School in Western Highlands Province have been suspended for one week following a violent The post Fight at Paglum Adventist Secondary School Leaves 16 Students Injured, Classes Suspended first appeared on Education News PNG

item.description: [Inside PNG] Read about Ipul Powaseu's journey of resilience and education as a disability rights advocate in the July Bilum of Brilliance Series initiative. item.title: PNG Firm Brave Group Partners Australian Company to Launch New Integrity and Security Division
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Brave Group Ltd CEO Wesley Roy Papua New Guinea is set to strengthen its governance and professional standards after PNG-owned Brave Group (PNG) Ltd entered into a strategic partnership with Australia's Pinnacle Integrity Pty Ltd to establish the country's first integrated Integrity, Cultural Risk a

item.title: Women in PNG Politics
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's political history highlights persistent gender inequality, with women facing challenges in attaining parliamentary representation, despite various reform efforts and initiatives aimed at increasing their visibility and participation. The post Women in PNG Politics first appeared o

item.title: Solomon Airlines Returns to Papua New Guinea, Reviving Regional Route After 15-Year Break
item.description: [PNG Facts] Regional air travel in the southwest Pacific has received a fresh boost with Solomon Airlines reinstating direct passenger services between Honiara and Port Moresby after an absence of 15 years, restoring one of Melanesia's key aviation links. The relaunch coincided with celebrations marking the Sol

item.title: Trio to face National Court for Card Cloning Fraud
item.description: [Post Courier] Three Chimbu men have been committed to the National Court to stand trial for card cloning in Lae, Morobe Province, on Monday-July 07 th . item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape says the Papua New Guinea–Australia Defence Treaty marks a new chapter in the relationship between the two countries, elevating it beyond a traditional bilateral partnership into a broader strategic partnership founded on shared history, democratic values, economic cooper

item.title: TSC advertises 42, 000 teaching positions for 2027 school year
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Teaching Service Commission (TSC) has advertised 42,000 teaching positions nationwide for the 2027 academic year and directed eligible teachers and graduating education students to submit applications by July 31. Acting TSC Chairman Maini Mike Ugaia announced yesterday in a statement that the va

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Pukpuk Treaty: PNG and Australia Officially Become Allies
item.description: [Inside PNG] PAPUA NEW GUINEA and Australia are now, formally allies with the, entry into force of the Pukpuk Treaty. This means the articles outlined in the Bilateral Security Deal are now legally binding and can be implemented. The announcement made on 11th July by both Prime Ministers will strengthen the inte

item.title: PNG names Dr Nicole Haley to lead Electoral Commission
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has announced Dr Nicole Haley as Papua New Guinea's new Electoral Commissioner, entrusting the election specialist with overseeing preparations for the country's 2027 National General Election. The appointment follows weeks of speculation over the position and comes after

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. The post Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG PM Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific Free From Military Weapons Testing
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has appealed to China and other military powers to stop using the Pacific Ocean for missile tests and weapons trials after a strategic ballistic missile was launched into international waters in the South Pacific. Describing the Pacific as a region that should be prot

item.title: Kina Bank supports IBBM’s 72nd Graduation
item.description: [Post Courier] Kina Bank has reaffirmed its commitment to building skills, leadership and opportunity in Papua New Guinea through its support as a major sponsor of the Institute of Banking and Business Management (IBBM) 72nd Graduation Ceremony, where the bank’s Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director Mr Iva

item.title: Water PNG targets July 31 for major pipeline repairs, warns of reduced water supply
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Water PNG has announced that it is targeting the evening of Friday, 31 July, to begin emergency repair works on the ageing Rouna 1/3 raw water pipeline, with customers across Port Moresby expected to experience significantly reduced water supply for up to three days. In a statement released yesterda

item.title: Solomon Airlines launches special airfare sale with new Port Moresby-Honiara service; one-way K980, return K2030
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Solomon Airlines has launched a special international airfare sale to mark the commencement of its new Port Moresby-Honiara service and the Solomon Islands’ 48th Independence anniversary, offering discounted fares for travel between the two Melanesian nations. The promotion was announced on Monday a

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process
item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.title: USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal
item.description: [Education News PNG] The 101st meeting of the Council of The University of the South Pacific (USP) was convened in Nukuʻalofa in the Kingdom of Tonga on 21–22 The post USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.description: [Inside PNG] The Maroons have lost their aura at Suncorp Stadium after a crushing 30-12 defeat in the decider. Queensland failed to handle the pressure. item.title: PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity The PNG Government has confirmed a review of its flagship SME Support Programme following concerns raised over the administration of K200 million in funding designed to support small business development nationwide. The initiative is part

item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. Enforcement is weak, with limited consequences for offenders, raising issues about electoral integrity and public trust. The post Early Campaigning in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG PM Marape Removes Minister Win Daki from Commerce Portfolio Pending SME Fund Probe
item.description: [PNG Facts] Prime Minister James Marape has stripped Minister Win Daki of the Commerce and Industry portfolio as the Government launches an independent investigation into allegations involving the administration of Papua New Guinea's Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund. The move also requires Commerce and In

item.title: Senior Bougainville Police Officer faces K3.5 million fraud and money laundering charges
item.description: [Post Courier] A high-ranking official with the Bougainville Police Service has been formally charged with 29 counts of corruption, fraud, and money laundering, involving more than K3.5 million in public funds. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Thre

item.title: K239 million highway upgrade to benefit Highlands region
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By ESWIN PLESIO THE people of Goroka in Eastern Highlands Province and the wider Highlands region will soon benefit from a major upgrade to a key section of the Highlands Highway following the signing of a K239.8 million contract between the Department of Works and Highways (DoWH) and China Wuyi Com

item.title: PM Marape urges Military Powers to keep Pacific an ‘Ocean of Peace’ after China missile test
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has called on all major military powers to respect the Pacific as an “Ocean of Peace” following China’s test launch of a strategic ballistic missile into international waters in the South Pacific. In a statement yesterday, PM Marape said the region should never become

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.description: [Inside PNG] PNGTPA and NCDC launch a landmark joint survey to boost tourism data, aiming to improve visitor experiences and track local tourism sector trends. item.title: PNG Health Access Strengthened Through Australia Aviation Partnership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG Health Access Strengthened Through Australia Aviation Partnership A new partnership between Australia and Adventist Aviation Services is set to improve the delivery of health services and essential supplies to remote communities throughout Papua New Guinea. The agreement will bolster the operati

item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. Strengthening oncology services and prevention efforts is essential to improve outcomes. The post Cancer Treatment in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV

item.title: PNG PM extends congratulations to US on 250 years of independence
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has sent his congratulations to United States President Donald J. Trump, his administration and the American people following celebrations marking 250 years of American independence on July 4. The Prime Minister described the milestone as a significant mo

item.title: South Bougainville MP backs National Education Conference in Buka with K100,000 boost
item.description: [Post Courier] In a significant gesture of solidarity ahead of Bougainville's anticipated independence, South Bougainville MP and Minister for Information and Communications Technology (ICT), Timothy Masiu, has contributed K100,000 to the Autonomous Bougainville Government's (ABG) Department of Education. To acces

item.title: Papua LNG Development Forum enters consultation phase as formal launch set for Friday
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By DALCY LULUA THE Papua LNG Development Forum has commenced in Port Moresby, with its formal launch scheduled for Friday (10/07/26), Petroleum Minister Jimmy Maladina announced yesterday. Speaking on the progress of the forum, Mr Maladina said the Government had originally planned to launch the for

item.title: Wapenamanda DDA pays K131, 000 in School Fee support for POM Business College students
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Wapenamanda District Development Authority (DDA) has paid K131,000 in school fee subsidies for Wapenamanda students attending Port Moresby Business College. This reaffirmed the DDA’s commitment to education as a key priority under the Kaeok Administration. Related news: https://thepngbulletin.co

item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] PNGEC i wari long hevi bilong sekyuriti na i surukim bai-ileksen bilong Dei Open i go bek tupela de

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.description: [Inside PNG] Australian employees are turning to artificial intelligence or AI tools to get ahead at work without the knowledge or permission of their employers. item.title: PNG Aviation Network to Benefit from New Niusky Investments and Leadership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea's air traffic management system is poised for further improvement as Niusky Pacific Limited advances plans to modernise its national aviation infrastructure under a newly appointed board of directors. With fresh leadership now in place, the state-owned air navigation service provide

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: Reshuffle looms for PNG Government as Marape tightens performance checks
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's Cabinet lineup is set for an overhaul, with Prime Minister James Marape signalling that a reshuffle will be announced within days. Speaking ahead of the expected Thursday or Friday announcement, the Prime Minister said the move stems from a lengthy review of how well ministers hav

item.title: A new era: Historic Australia-PNG Mutual Defence Treaty enters into force
item.description: [Post Courier] Prime Minister of Australia, Anthony Albanese MP, and Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, James Marape MP, have announced the entry into force of the Pukpuk Treaty. item.title: PPL to appeal Dirio Court Decision
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PNG Power Limited (PPL) will appeal the National Court’s decision delivered on 7 July in relation to a claim brought by Dirio Gas & Power Limited against PPL, Kumul Consolidated Holdings (KCH) and the State. PNG Power Chief Executive Officer Paul Bayly said the company would review the details of th

item.title: New Ireland government commits K100, 000 to AFL PNG Youth Development
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The New Ireland Provincial Government has committed K100,000 to AFL Papua New Guinea to expand grassroots football programs and create more opportunities for young people through sport across the province. Governor Byron Chan announced the funding partnership at Colts Oval in Port Moresby this week,

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: Hunters Hunted Down: Seagulls Maul PNG Hunters in 54-0 Thrashing
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Hunters were thumped 54-0 by Wynnum Manly Seagulls in a Round 16 clash played at BMD Kougari Oval in Brisbane on Sunday, with the home side crossing for ten tries in a comprehensive victory over the visitors. Hunters Hunted Down: Seagulls Maul PNG in 54-0 Thrashing The Seagulls set the tone earl

To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three

item.title: Australia launches K760 million Pacific Rugby League Partnership
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] AUSTRALIA has announced a new $250 million (K760m), 10-year Pacific Rugby League Partnership, aimed at strengthening rugby league across the Pacific while creating opportunities in education, leadership and employment. Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese joined Papua New Guinea Prime Minister

item.title: PNG’s Economic Growth stays strong: Westpac reports
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Westpac’s latest PNG Economic Update and Outlook says PNG’s growth outlook remains strong, supported by ongoing activity in agriculture, industry and services. The report forecasts real GDP growth of around 4.6 per cent in 2026, following estimated growth of 5.5 per cent in 2025. Related news: https

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation
item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: PNG PM UNVEILS K316 MILLION INFRASTRUCTURE DRIVE FOR GULF
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has rolled out a K316 million infrastructure package targeting roads, bridges and community facilities for rural Gulf Province residents, part of a broader push to reach the majority of citizens living outside urban centres. Standing before villagers gath

item.title: U.S. Embassy Port Moresby announces Exercise Tamiok Strike 2026
item.description: [Post Courier] The United States Army Pacific and the Papua New Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF) will conduct Exercise Tamiok Strike 2026 from July 13–24, 2026, at locations including Port Moresby, Wewak, and Lae. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three

item.title: GoPNG and QUT sign strategic partnership to accelerate national digital transformation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Government of Papua New Guinea (GoPNG) today signed a landmark Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Queensland University of Technology (QUT), establishing a strategic partnership to support Papua New Guinea’s National Digital Transformation Agenda. The MoU was signed by the Acting Ministe

item.title: PNG’s Forex flow has improved: Westpac report says
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Foreign exchange liquidity in PNG has improved significantly, helping businesses and supporting private sector lending, according to the Westpac’s latest PNG Economic Update and Outlook. The report says IMF-led reforms and stronger resource export earnings have helped reduce PNG’s long-running forei

item.title: Digicel PNG Declares Network Ready for 5G Rollout Following Successful Trials
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has moved a step closer to the next generation of mobile communications after Digicel PNG announced its network has successfully passed end-to-end 5G readiness testing, confirming the system is prepared to support future 5G services. The telecommunications company said trial results

item.title: PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman PNG Prime Minister James Marape has confirmed the appointment of Tabitha Suwae as Acting Chief Ombudsman following the completion of Richard Pagen's six-year term, with the Government beginning the process of recruiting a permanent head for
